text:
Once upon a very quiet Tuesday, there was a girl named Lucy who lived in a tiny town famous only for giant sequoia trees. She loved visiting the forest behind her house. On a fine spring day, she spotted something sparkling on the ground. Curious, she knelt down.

It was a tiny sequoia tree. Its bark was smoother than any forest giant Lucy knew. She took it home and named it Sapphire.

Sapphire grew quickly. Lucy realized it was a beautiful tree with a soft green hue and tiny golden leaves. She spent her weekends gently watering and playing with Sapphire. They sang together under the big gum trees.

One summer afternoon, as Lucy sat resting on a hillside, she spotted something unusual. A squirrel passed by, nose wrinkled, carrying something shiny. Lucy chased it down a sunlit glade.

There was a small crystal globe. Curious, Lucy placed it on the ground. Suddenly, Sapphire began to glow. In a soft voice,
